task.title: Write a Project Plan Document
task.shortdesc: Use the project plan editor to capture outlines, research notes, diagrams, and any reference material that supports your work on a project.

context.p: Each project includes a Markdown plan document. The editor offers a preview panel that renders formatted text and Mermaid diagrams. All changes save automatically as you type.

step.cmd: Click the Projects tab and select the project you want to edit.

step.stepresult: The Project dialog opens. The lower half of the dialog shows the plan document in preview mode.

step.cmd: Click Edit to switch the plan document to edit mode.
step.stepresult: A Markdown text editor replaces the preview panel.

step.cmd: Write your plan document using standard Markdown syntax.

info.p: The plan document has no required structure. Common approaches include:

ul.li: An outline or table of contents
Tables of characters, chapters, modules, or tasks

info.p: To include a Mermaid diagram, use a fenced code block with the mermaid language tag:
info.codeblock: ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Draft] --> B[Revision] B --> C[Final] ```

step.cmd: Click Preview to review the rendered output.
step.stepresult: The editor switches to preview mode. Markdown formatting and Mermaid diagrams render automatically. Click Edit to return to edit mode at any time.

result.p: Your plan document is saved automatically. There is no separate save action for plan document content.

postreq.p: See for the full list of supported Markdown elements and Mermaid diagram types.
